content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA  in sorted order, and QQQ queries that contain iii and xxx for each query you have to find smallest j such that j≥ij \geq ij≥i and ∑k=ijAk≥x\sum_{k=i}^j A_k \geq x∑k=ij​Ak​≥x. If the answer doesn’t exist, report −1-1−1 as the value of jjj.


Input Format

The first line of input contains 2 integers NNN - size of array AAA and QQQ - number of queries
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,……,ANA_1, A_2,……, A_NA1​,A2​,……,AN​
Each of the next QQQ lines contains a 2 integer iii and xxx for each query.


Output Format

Output QQQ space-separated integers with mthm^{th}mth integer as jjj value for mthm^{th}mth query.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains two integers N and Q. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN (the array is given in non‑decreasing order). Each of the next Q lines contains two integers i and x describing a query.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output Q space‑separated integers on a single line. The m‑th integer is the smallest index j (j ≥ i) such that the sum A_i + A_{i+1} + … + A_j ≥ x. If no such j exists output -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For the only test case:
Query1 i=1,x=5 → A1=10≥5 so j=1.
Query2 i=2,x=60 → sum(2..3)=50<60, sum(2..4)=90≥60 so j=4.
Query3 i=1,x=149 → sum(1..5)=150≥149 so j=5.
Query4 i=1,x=151 → total sum=150<151, no j exists → -1.

solution.approach: For each test case compute a prefix sum array P where P[0]=0 and P[k]=A1+…+Ak (64‑bit). For a query (i,x) we need the smallest j≥i with P[j]‑P[i‑1] ≥ x, i.e. P[j] ≥ P[i‑1]+x. Because P is strictly increasing (all A_i>0) we can binary‑search the first position ≥ target in the range [i, N]. If such position exists answer=j, otherwise answer=-1. The binary search is O(log N) per query, giving O(N+Q log N) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N + Q·log N) per test case
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the prefix sum array

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = list(map(int, sys.stdin.read().strip().split()))
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = next(it)
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = next(it); Q = next(it)
        pref = [0]
        for _ in range(N):
            pref.append(pref[-1] + next(it))
        answers = []
        for _ in range(Q):
            i = next(it); x = next(it)
            target = pref[i-1] + x
            # binary search in pref[i:]
            lo, hi = i, N
            ans = -1
            while lo <= hi:
                mid = (lo + hi) // 2
                if pref[mid] >= target:
                    ans = mid
                    hi = mid - 1
                else:
                    lo = mid + 1
            answers.append(str(ans if ans != -1 else -1))
        out_lines.append(' '.join(answers))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
